# Samsung will give you $300 for any old tablet if you buy Galaxy Tab S10 – here’s how to get an extra $100

[![Avatar for Ben Schoon](https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/59cb0e121a572a78cfc8040731068741887591b7ef93f5498a8d93310278c0f7?s=30&d=mm&r=r)](https://9to5google.com/author/nexusben1/) [Ben Schoon](https://9to5google.com/author/nexusben1/ "Posts by Ben Schoon") \| Nov 5 2024 - 10:30 am PT

[9 Comments](https://9to5google.com/2024/11/05/samsung-galaxy-tab-s10-trade-in-deal/#comments)

![](https://9to5google.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/2024/11/galaxy-tab-s10-1.jpg?quality=82&strip=all&w=1200)

If you’re looking to ditch an old tablet for something new, Samsung will give you $300 for any old tablet trade towards its new Galaxy Tab S10 series, and you can score an additional $100 off right here.

The [Galaxy Tab S10+ and Galaxy Tab S10 Ultra](https://howl.link/6qdj3sfj84uqn) – there is no base Galaxy Tab S10 – launched in September with the same display sizes and many of the same features, but added emphasis on AI features (including an “AI Key” on the keyboard) as well as a new anti-reflective display.

At their usual starting prices of $999 and $1,199, they’re some of the best Android tablets on the market, but Samsung has a great deal available right now.

Through Samsung.com exclusively, you can get a [Galaxy Tab S10+ or Tab S10 Ultra](https://howl.link/6qdj3sfj84uqn) for _any_ old Android tablet. As long as the tablet is in good condition, turning on and without a cracked display, you’ll [get $300 for it](https://howl.link/6qdj3sfj84uqn). You can also score up to $800 towards either tablet with the trade-in of a Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra, or around $700 for other recent Samsung flagships or select iPad models.

Advertisement - scroll for more content

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Towards Galaxy Tab S10+ | Towards Galaxy Tab S10 Ultra |
| Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra | $700 | $800 |
| Galaxy Tab S8 Ultra | $650 | $750 |
| Galaxy Tab S9+ | $600 | $700 |
| Galaxy Tab S8+ | $500 | $600 |
| Galaxy Tab S9 | $450 | $500 |
| Galaxy Tab S6 Lite | $300 | $300 |
| iPad Pro 12.9″ 6th-Gen | $600 | $700 |
| iPad Pro 11″ 3rd-Gen | $400 | $400 |
| iPad Air 5th-Gen | $350 | $350 |
| Any Working Android Tablet | $300 | $300 |

On top of that, Samsung offers 30% off when you bundle in a Galaxy Watch or Galaxy Buds.

You can also get an additional $100 [when you use _9to5Google’s_ exclusive link](https://howl.link/6qdj3sfj84uqn), with the additional $100 available as a credit towards other devices/accessories. That bonus is only available through November 7, though.

![](https://9to5google.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/2024/11/image_213352.png?w=1024)

## More on Samsung:

- [Samsung’s Galaxy AI reportedly getting notification summary feature in One UI 7](https://9to5google.com/2024/11/04/samsungs-galaxy-ai-getting-notification-summary/)
- [Latest One UI 7 leak reveals Samsung features and new lock screen coming with Android 15](https://9to5google.com/2024/10/30/samsung-one-ui-7-features-leak/)
- [Samsung plans new foldables including tablet as Galaxy Z Fold 6, Flip 6 sales struggle](https://9to5google.com/2024/10/23/samsung-foldable-expansion-report-fold-6-flip-6-sales/)

_Update: The extra $100 is a credit available for additional accessories or devices, not an extra $100 off of the tablet itself._
